summary: Closed without merge. The PR body claimed 24 item functions decompiled across multiple item files, with `ninja`, `ninja diff`, and objdiff 100% checks passing. The visible diff replaces 20 `/// #` item stubs, updates item struct/header typing in `itCommonItems.h`, and adds substantial Claude Code automation/tooling docs and scripts. No review comments or closure rationale are present in the slice.
what_changed: Replaced visible item stubs for `it_3F14_Logic7_Thrown`, `it_3F14_Logic7_DmgDealt`, `itGreatfoxlaser_UnkMotion1_Phys`, `it_802C9D40`, `itKabigon_UnkMotion0_Anim`, `it_802CA3F4`, `itLugia_UnkMotion4_Phys`, `it_2725_Logic102_Destroyed`, `itNesspkthundertrail_UnkMotion0_Anim`, `it_802BE5D8`, `it_802BE958`, `itOldottosea_UnkMotion7_Coll`, `it_802E0D9C`, `it_802B43D0`, `itTincle_UnkMotion4_Phys`, `itTincle_UnkMotion7_Coll`, `itWhitebea_UnkMotion1_Anim`, `it_802E3DA0`, `it_802E4464`, and `it_2725_Logic9_Dropped`. The PR title/body say 24 functions, but this dump's diff evidence exposes 20 stub replacements.

Updated item data typing in `itCommonItems.h`: `itLugiaAttributes` gained `x14`/`x18`, `itDosei_ItemVars.xDE0` changed from `s32` to `f32`, `itPokemon_ItemVars` gained unions for integer/float access plus `xE48`, `itTincleAttributes` was introduced with fields at `0x44`/`0x48`, and White Bea vars/attrs gained `x44`/`x10` fields.

Replaced several header `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` declarations with typed prototypes, including `it_802CA074(Item_GObj*)`, `it_802D1A44(Item_GObj*)`, `it_802E0D9C(Item_GObj*)`, and `it_802B43D0(Item_GObj*, HSD_GObj*)`.

Implemented item behavior using existing game helpers: item state changes via `Item_80268E5C`, physics/collision helpers, `HSD_JObj` visibility/animation calls, owner cleanup for Ness/Kirby PK Flash and Ness yoyo, random offsets/effects for Kabigon, and map collision facing updates for White Bea.
Added `CLAUDE.md` with project build, style, naming, decomp workflow, objdiff, and permuter guidance.

Added autonomous decompilation scripts for scanning stubs, extracting asm/m2c context, batching small targets, running Claude Code in worktrees, parsing stream-json results, tracking progress, rate-limit handling, optional PR creation, and macOS launchd scheduling.
smart_moves: The item implementations lean on typed item vars/attributes instead of raw pointer arithmetic; the new unions in `itPokemon_ItemVars` preserve access to the same storage as either integer or float, which is a common matching need without making fake casts the primary representation.
Headers were tightened where implementations exposed true signatures, reducing future `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` debt and making call sites easier to type-match.

Several functions preserve framework side effects rather than only reproducing local arithmetic: PK Flash destruction clears the owner reference and calls the Ness/Kirby reset helpers; yoyo cleanup walks linked item objects and removes them; White Bea direction changes update both `facing_dir` and collision facing.
The diff uses existing HSD/item helpers for visibility, animation state, collision callbacks, effects, and sounds, which is usually more robust than matching with ad hoc stores.
Matching-specific padding was added only where needed in visible implementations (`PAD_STACK(8)` appears in several functions), signaling stack-frame comparison was part of the match process.

The added automation scripts separate helper logic into testable Python subcommands and parse Claude stream JSON result events instead of grepping conversational output, which is a sound pattern if similar automation is ever accepted.
decomp_lessons: Before decompiling item callbacks, audit `itCommonItems.h` for the correct per-item variable layout and attribute field types; small `s32` vs `f32` mistakes can cascade into load/store mismatches.
Use unions for item variable storage when assembly shows the same offset being consumed as both integer and float in different contexts.
When replacing stubs, update neighboring headers at the same time so future callers stop depending on `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS`.
For fighter-owned item projectiles, match teardown ownership semantics, not just the visible item state transition; owner pointers and fighter-side flags often need clearing.
For motion/collision callbacks, preserve state-machine helper calls (`Item_80268E5C`, collision callbacks, `it_8027C794`, etc.) and the exact order of velocity/facing updates.

The PR is closed and unmerged with no reviewer rationale in the slice, so treat its code and tooling as reference material requiring re-verification against current master rather than accepted project precedent.
naming_conventions: Item functions follow the project pattern of file/module-prefixed names when known, such as `itGreatfoxlaser_UnkMotion1_Phys`, `itLugia_UnkMotion4_Phys`, and `itWhitebea_UnkMotion1_Anim`.
Unidentified functions remain address-style `it_802...` symbols, such as `it_802C9D40`, `it_802CA3F4`, `it_802BE958`, and `it_802E4464`.
Logic callback names preserve item-system naming like `it_2725_Logic102_Destroyed`, `it_2725_Logic9_Dropped`, and `it_3F14_Logic7_DmgDealt`.

`CLAUDE.md` documents project style: file names use lowerCamelCase, function names use UpperCamelCase with module/file prefixes, standard library reimplementations use lowercase, and unknown struct fields use offset/x-style names.
Struct fields in the diff use offset comments and x-prefixed unknown names, e.g. `/* +44 ip+E18 */ s32 x44;` and `/* 0x48 */ f32 x48;`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: PR body test plan claims `ninja` builds successfully, `ninja diff` has no regressions, and all functions are 100% matches in objdiff.
Visible implementations use `PAD_STACK(8)` in functions where stack-frame matching apparently required it.
Header and struct typing changes are part of the matching tactic: concrete `Item_GObj*` signatures and `f32`/`s32` field choices steer codegen toward the original loads, stores, and call ABI.

The added `CLAUDE.md` explicitly recommends true matches over fake pointer arithmetic, avoiding unnecessary `goto`, using `for` loops for normal loops, using `ABS`/`MIN`/`MAX` for common patterns, and ignoring pure register swaps until permuter work.
The added automation workflow extracts function assembly, includes m2c output, builds only the target object with Ninja, checks `report.json`, and iterates on exact byte/instruction differences.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews are present in the provided PR slice.
No evidence is provided for why the PR was closed without merge.
follow_up_queries: Check current master for each visible function name to determine which item decompilations were later merged in other PRs.
Investigate why PR #2277 was closed without merge; this slice has no comments or reviews explaining it.
Resolve the count discrepancy: PR title/body claim 24 item functions, while the provided diff exposes 20 removed `/// #` stub markers.
Audit whether the added `CLAUDE.md` and overnight decomp scripts were ever accepted, superseded, or intentionally excluded from the project.
Before reusing the struct changes, compare `itCommonItems.h` on current master for `itPokemon_ItemVars`, `itDosei_ItemVars`, `itTincleAttributes`, `itLugiaAttributes`, and `itWhiteBea*` layouts.
